RO1_eating_moments.text: Oh, it's like an unspoken ritual... We're not meant to call it a 'ritual,' but it definitely is.

Honestly, like, I didn't cook until I came to uni. It's definitely not a passion of mine at all. Cooking means I'm so hungry, I just want to eat. But other than that... nothing. I don't really get creative with my meals. I don't do anything special. It's just enough to, like, fulfill me.

So I was with one of my housemates. Everyone else had gone out like, like a night out and we, we were just going to do like a movie night in, watch a film, get some sweets and stuff. Probably order a little bit more recently... But then, yeah, when you sort of get into the year, you kind of just sort of like, 'I just want that,' and they'll bring it to my house in, like, 20 minutes.
I'm definitely more of eating out person or, you know, getting takeaway person that I am a cooking person. Although I'm trying to change that this year.

So, I do enjoy cooking, but I don't have the time to do it. So, I like to throw in the pan myself... cook... cook a nice dinner, like spaghetti bolognese or a curry. Like, I like cooking myself, but I just don't have the time to do it.

None are really that stressful. Like my lunches are pretty like low, like intervention, I don't have to do much for them. And I also have a terrible habit of basically just making the same thing for months at a time until I get sick of it and then just change something else.
It's necessary. It's not enjoyable.

So now I'd say I'd go like maybe once every like four days, which I just find much more manageable. I don't, I don't love food shopping, but I hate throwing away food more than I hate food shopping.

So today, I skipped my breakfast again because being a student, it is, like, one of the most common habit for me that I skipped my breakfast on a regular basis because I had lectures early in the morning, so I had to go to the campus to attend the lectures.

It's worth saying, but it's like... it's a full stress. Yes. It sort of takes you out from when you're... when you're doing, like, work all day. It's just something different that you can focus on. You're not really focusing on everything else with your cooking.

I've definitely spent more time cooking for sure, but also because I own the whole kitchen right now, cooking becomes way more enjoyable. I'm not limited to fridge space as well, which is, which is great. Like, I don't want to be traveling four hours a day and all that.

I would see it as trying to get proper, proper fuel because I have such a hectic weeks and all that like food and having really good meals I know is only going to help, not hinder, I suppose, my energy and whatnot.
